title: Baroli Temple Complex
tagline: Baroli Temple Complex — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Baroli Temples Complex, also known as the Badoli temples, is located in Baroli village in Rawatbhata City in Chittorgarh district of Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: The Baroli Temples Complex, also known as the Badoli temples, is located in Baroli village in Rawatbhata City in Chittorgarh district of Rajasthan, India. The complex of eight temples is situated within a walled enclosure; an additional temple is about 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) away. They are built in the Gurjara Pratihara style of temple architecture dated to the tenth century CE and today show various degree of preservation, decay and destruction. All nine temples are under the control of the Archaeological Survey of India for conservation and protection.

topPlaces.name: Ghateshwar Temple
topPlaces.description: Ghateshwar Temple is a Hindu temple located in Badoli (also spelled Baroli), one of the nine temples near the river Chambal in Rajasthan, India. The Ghateshwar temple is dedicated to the god Shiva and is constructed in the Pratihara style of circa.

topPlaces.name: Rawatbhata
topPlaces.description: Rawatbhata is a city, Tehsil headquarter, Sub District headquarter and Nagar Palika in Chittorgarh District, Rajasthan, Rawatbhata is also known as the nuclear city of Rajasthan and India's first nuclear city. Rawatbhata is a proposed district headquarter, it is 6th largest city in Udaipur Division.

topPlaces.name: Bhainsrorgarh
topPlaces.description: Bhainsrorgarh Fort or Bhainsror Fort is an ancient fort that has become a major tourist spot in the state of Rajasthan, India. The nearest City is Rawatbhata, 7 km from Bhainsror.

topPlaces.name: Rana Pratap Sagar Dam
topPlaces.description: The Rana Pratap Sagar Dam is a gravity masonry dam of 53.8 metres (177 ft) height built on the Chambal River at Rawatbhata in Rajasthan in India. It is part of integrated scheme of a cascade development of the river involving four projects starting with the Gandhi Sagar Dam in the upstream reach (48 kilometres (30 mi) upstream) in Madhya Pradesh and the Jawahar Sagar Dam on the downstream (28 kilometres (17 mi) downstream) with a terminal structure of the Kota Barrage (28 kilometres (17 mi) further downstream) in Rajasthan for irrigation.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in southeastern Rajasthan, India. It was notified on 9 October 1975 and covers an area of approximately 194.6 km2 (75.1 sq mi), spanning the districts of Kota, Bundi, and Chittorgarh along the Chambal River.

topPlaces.name: Rajasthan Atomic Power Station
topPlaces.description: The Rajasthan Atomic Power Station (RAPS; also, Rajasthan Atomic Power Project - RAPP) is a nuclear power plant located at Rawatbhata in the state of Rajasthan, India. It is the third largest nuclear power plant in the country after the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant and Kakrapar Atomic Power Station in terms of installed capacity.

items.note: The Rana Pratap Sagar Dam is a gravity masonry dam of 53.8 metres (177 ft) height built on the Chambal River at Rawatbhata in Rajasthan in India. It is part of integrated scheme of a cascade development of the river involving four projects starting with the Gandhi Sagar Dam in the upstream reach (48 kilometres (30 mi) upstream) in Madhya Pradesh and the Jawahar Sagar Dam on the downstream (28 kilometres (17 mi) downstream) with a terminal structure of the Kota Barrage (28 kilometres (17 mi) further downstream) in Rajasthan for irrigation.

Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in southeastern Rajasthan, India. It was notified on 9 October 1975 and covers an area of approximately 194.6 km2 (75.1 sq mi), spanning the districts of Kota, Bundi, and Chittorgarh along the Chambal River.

The Rajasthan Atomic Power Station (RAPS; also, Rajasthan Atomic Power Project - RAPP) is a nuclear power plant located at Rawatbhata in the state of Rajasthan, India. It is the third largest nuclear power plant in the country after the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant and Kakrapar Atomic Power Station in terms of installed capacity.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baroli Temple Complex?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baroli Temple Complex is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baroli Temple Complex?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kota Airport (34 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (36 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baroli Temple Complex?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baroli Temple Complex at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baroli Temple Complex?
faq.a: Stays in Baroli Temple Complex start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baroli Temple Complex?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghateshwar Temple, Rawatbhata, Bhainsrorgarh, Rana Pratap Sagar Dam, Jawahar Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary.
